phase3_findings.description: _run_git decodes git show output with the locale-default codec (text=True); a non-ASCII file body under a C/POSIX locale raises UnicodeDecodeError that propagates to exit 2, masking every other file's verdict incl. a real STALE.
Phase 0.6 helper-run fence captured no exit status, so an exit-2 internal error (empty stdout) was indistinguishable from 'no stale claims' — arm (c) inert (policy-without-mechanism).
R2 reconciled sibling asserted only exit 0, no VERIFIED positive control — a mutant dropping legend-block detection would stay GREEN.
R1 _forward_maxcase scans to EOF; an unrelated later 'Case N' in a different section could mark a 'Cases A-B' header STALE (false positive).
parse_diff misclassifies an added content line beginning with '++ ' as a '+++' file header (low likelihood in prose).
git show rc!=0 uniformly maps to UNRESOLVABLE; a genuine internal git error on one file is silently reclassified rather than exit 2. Blast radius low (rev pre-validated).

config.schema.json stale_prose description says 'Phase-3 pass of /devflow:review-and-fix' — matches the pre-existing line-202 idiom in the same file; changing only the new line would create inconsistency.

fix_decisions.claim_text: R1 forward scan to EOF false positives across sections
fix_decisions.evidence: By-design per issue AC (whole-forward-region scan); false positives handled by the config severity knob and the fix_decisions pushback valve, which are the issue's intended valves. Narrowing to block boundaries would be a design change beyond scope.
Very low likelihood in prose (an added line whose content literally begins '++ '); fixing risks new parser edge cases for a Suggestion-level latent bug.

fix_decisions.claim_text: git show rc!=0 -> UNRESOLVABLE collapses genuine git errors
fix_decisions.evidence: Blast radius low: --rev is pre-validated up front, so the common 'path not at rev' case is legitimately UNRESOLVABLE; a genuine internal git failure on one file is rare.

Line 202 (verdict_severity_threshold, pre-existing, not in this PR's diff) already uses the identical 'Phase-3 pass of /devflow:review-and-fix' idiom; the new line 213 matches that established convention. Changing only 213 introduces intra-file inconsistency; changing 202 too is out-of-scope.

phase3_findings.description: R4 has no VERIFIED positive control (dead VERIFIED arm; mutant forcing R4 always-STALE ships green)

post_shadow_edit_gate.delta_review: clean (blinded general-purpose delta-reviewer over b00d28f..HEAD found no new Critical/Important); reviewed_sha advanced to HEAD
